companion_note: HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-PIPELINE-ROUTING-001 (in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json) — strict decider holdpoint behaviour
target: New top-level "Scoreboard" tab + per-row scoreboard object schema extensions to data/wins.json, data/predictions.json, and a new data/killshots.json. Phase 1 covers 15 rows (9 claim_index-classified WINs/predictions + 6 kill-shots); Phase 2 extends to the full row set.

required_pipeline_path.step_1_analyst: Produce this EXP with status=DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW (DONE — this file)
required_pipeline_path.step_2_decider: Route to curmudgeon with REQUIRE_ADVERSARIAL_REVIEW; DO NOT auto-integrate; create tracking issue ISS-NNN; log chg-*.json change file (per companion routing HNOTE)
required_pipeline_path.step_3_curmudgeon: Adversarial review (for human consumption; does NOT by itself authorize integration)
required_pipeline_path.step_4_HOLD: Pipeline stops. Operator + human group iterate on draft + curmudgeon review.
required_pipeline_path.step_5_conditional_operator: If and only if HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-001 is posted with approved_curmudgeon_review_id, decider may integrate subject to technical gates (i)-(iv) from companion routing HNOTE.
required_pipeline_path.step_6_post_integration: Update poller.md load-bearing-page list; update CLAUDE.md Architecture; log chg-*.json strategic/HIGH.

schema_extension_proposal.description: Per-row scoreboard object attached to each WIN / prediction / kill-shot. Backwards-compatible: absent scoreboard object renders as "—" blanks.

fields.globe_prediction_value: number | string | null — authoritative globe-model prediction
fields.globe_prediction_source: string | null — named authoritative reference (e.g., "IGRF-14", "Chapman 1931", "IAU SOFA", "WGS84 + Vincenty")
fields.dome_prediction_as_published_value: number | string | null — BLANK if author did not publish a number for this row. NEVER derived by us.
fields.dome_prediction_as_published_source: string | null — verbatim citation (e.g., "claim_index.json#win-069", "V51.1 paper eq. 4.7")
fields.dome_prediction_as_published_quote: string | null — verbatim quote of the sentence/equation
fields.dome_prediction_as_published_is_qualitat: boolean — true when claim is qualitative only ("phenomenon predicted"), no number
fields.dome_prediction_formula_check_value: number | string | null — OPTIONAL independent implementation of V51.1 derivation
fields.dome_prediction_formula_check_implementa: string | null — path to script in build-scripts/scoreboard-formulas/
fields.dome_prediction_formula_check_inputs_are: boolean | null — FALSE if formula runs on globe-native inputs with near-identity transform
fields.formula_reproduces: boolean | null — published value within rounding tolerance of formula-check?
fields.provenance_note: string | null — one-sentence explanation with source
fields.observation_is_pending: boolean — true for future-window observations (e.g., Aug 12 2026 eclipse)
fields.win_by_own_standard: "yes" | "no" | "blank" | "pending" | "disputed" — computed ONLY from published dome value vs observation. Null dome value => "blank".
fields.discriminating: "yes" | "no" | "blank" | "pending" | "disputed" — computed ONLY from published dome value vs globe value. Null dome value => "blank".
fields.row_notes: string | null — analyst prose, not scored

applies_to: data/predictions.json entries (96 rows; only prediction/tracking entry_type apply)
data/killshots.json entries (NEW file — 6 kill-shot rows drawn from part5 narrative)

columns: dome prediction AS PUBLISHED (value ± unc) + source/quote
build_render_contract.blank_rendering: — (em-dash), no inferred score, footnote states "the author has not published a specific dome prediction for this row"

phase1_content.description: 15 rows populated using author's published materials (claim_index.json, wins.html, killshot.html, coordinates.html, predictions.html). Rule 1 honoured — where the dome author has NOT published a specific numerical value, dome_prediction_as_published_value is null and win_by_own_standard/discriminating are "blank".

globe.source: Geoscience Australia cadastral geodesy; WGS84 + Vincenty great-circle over the road network

dome_published.quote: V13 two-zone topology predicts the Nullarbor 1.00 control while also predicting the Sydney-Perth excess and the broader Australia scaffold fit.

formula_check.value: 4352 km (derived from V13 Finsler λ_g profile with Sydney-Perth input)

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: The V13 λ_g profile contains free parameters fit to the Australia corridor dataset INCLUDING Sydney-Perth. Per EXP-085/EXP-091 archival and wins.json WIN-069, Sydney-Perth is both an input to the parameter fit AND the claimed prediction. This is the canonical fit case (Math-wrong CASE B).
scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: WIN credited by published = observation by author's own standard. Non-discriminating: dome value equals globe value within rounding (both 4,352 km). Provenance FIT: number was input to the λ_g fit, not predicted independently. Reading A / Reading B bifurcation makes the label "retrospective_structural" concrete on this row: the structure doing the work is globe structure.

globe.value: Chapman-Sq ionospheric response: ΔB amplitudes in the −5 to −25 nT range at mid-latitudes during totality, with spatial pattern tracking ionospheric recovery (~10–30 min time constant).
globe.source: Chapman 1931 + Hargreaves 1979; replicated in modern Sq modelling by the INTERMAGNET community.

dome_published.value: qualitative ("systematic magnetic response centered on eclipse geometry" across 39 eclipses / 207 manifestations / 100 observatories)
dome_published.quote: Peer-reviewed ensemble analysis across 39 eclipses, 207 manifestations, and 100 observatories shows a systematic magnetic response centered on eclipse geometry.

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: Dome claim is derived from standard ionospheric datasets (INTERMAGNET) with the claim "these exist and cluster on eclipse geometry". Chapman-Sq predicts the same clustering. The author himself flags this: honest_caveat in claim_index.json says "the site explicitly notes that ionospheric Sq disruption is a competing globe-compatible mechanism".

observation.value: Meta-analysis ensemble positive for eclipse-aligned signal at claimed scale
observation.source: Peer-reviewed ensemble study as cited on wins.html#win-068 (SRC-ECL-001, SRC-INTERMAGNET-001)

scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: Qualitative WIN credited. Non-discriminating per author's own honest_caveat. This row is strong evidence that signals EXIST; it is NOT evidence that the dome architecture explains them better than Chapman-Sq.

globe.value: No single-number globe prediction — standard gravimeter physics rejects a direct B/g linear coupling at this amplitude without a shared driver; the globe-side baseline is "no robust coupling observed in clean IGETS records"
globe.source: IGETS processed records; standard superconducting-gravimeter noise model

dome_published.source: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html#win-058-kappa; PRED-KAPPA-001; forensic reconstruction referenced in claim_index.json#win-058-bedrock-kappa
dome_published.quote: The claimed 1.67 nT per microGal coupling is now carried as pending because the current support depends on an internal forensic reconstruction not yet independently reproduced from raw L1A access.

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: κ is a dome-native coupling constant; it does not reduce to a standard-physics quantity under a near-identity transform. The author himself flags independent reproduction as required.

observation.value: pending — author's honest_caveat states "Independent raw-data reproduction or a successful August 12 2026 Tier 3 result is still required"
observation.source: IGETS L1A raw access requested; no independent reproduction as of 2026-04-17

scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: The author himself reclassified this to pending_independent_verification (claim_index.json 2026-04 rigor push). Scoreboard reflects his own status. If Aug 12 2026 Tier-3 null is observed, this row becomes no. Cross-links to pred-eclipse-tier3.

globe.value: Identity/convention: any polar-azimuthal projection (including the AE projection commonly used in flat-earth diagrams) satisfies θ = −lonE when azimuth is measured from the same reference meridian with opposite sign convention.
globe.source: Standard cartography: Snyder, Map Projections (USGS PP 1395); IAU SOFA.

dome_published.value: θ = −lonE (asserted as unified angular coordinate rule)
dome_published.quote: Unified angular coordinate rule for the dome frame: theta equals the negation of east longitude.

formula_check.value: θ = −lonE verified as identity under polar-azimuthal projection class

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: Every polar-centered projection satisfies this identity by construction (EXP-187 Mode-3 result). The claim does not discriminate dome from any globe polar-azimuthal projection.
scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: Credited by the author's own standard (the equation holds). Non-discriminating: polar-centered geometry is architectural; any polar-azimuthal projection satisfies the identity. See EXP-187 for the Advocate-defense analysis.

globe.value: No gravity anomaly at totality above LaCoste-Romberg instrument systematics (~5-7 µGal); mainstream reanalyses attribute the ~6 µGal signal to thermal tilt.
globe.source: Wang et al. 2000 reanalysis; standard LaCoste-Romberg temperature-coefficient characterization.

dome_published.value: Contact-phase-locked gravity anomaly (~6 µGal magnitude reported; the author's framing focuses on the TIMING of the anomaly relative to C1/C4 contacts)
dome_published.quote: The site now preserves both the contact-phase timing argument and the strongest conventional critiques: shielding is ruled out, and thermal tilt is not fully closed for the LaCoste-Romberg instrument.

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: Provenance unclear: the author presents the claim as an empirical observation (1997 data) rather than a V51.1 prediction. κ (WIN-058-bedrock-kappa) is a downstream claim that would use the Mohe observation as a data point.

observation.value: ~6 µGal anomaly with contact-phase-locked timing in Wang et al. 1997 data
observation.source: Wang et al. 1997 Mohe gravity observations, subsequent reanalyses

scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: Author's own claim_index "confirmed_mechanism_contested" — he is transparent that thermal tilt is not fully closed. WIN-by-own-standard disputed because the "confirmation" is of a contested mechanism, not a pre-registered prediction. This is the historical anchor for pred-eclipse-tier3.

globe.value: tan(elevation) = sin(φ_sun − φ_obs) depending on date/latitude; standard spherical astronomy returns observed elevations to arcsecond accuracy.
globe.source: IAU SOFA; Meeus Astronomical Algorithms; any ephemeris library (PyEphem, Skyfield).

dome_published.value: Solar elevation reproduced as "zero-free-parameter consequence of the H(r) structure and the same λ_g scale reused elsewhere"
dome_published.unc: not quantified as a per-row residual
dome_published.quote: Solar elevation is presented as a zero-free-parameter consequence of the H(r) structure and the same lambda_g scale reused elsewhere in the site.

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: The H(r) + λ_g derivation takes observed solar position and observer latitude/longitude as inputs. Because the projection maps globe lat/lon to disc (r,θ) under a fixed conformal rule, reproducing globe elevations from disc coordinates is algebraically required — not evidence of independent dome mechanics. Globe-pollution candidate; flagged G2/G4.

observation.value: solar elevations as measured to arcminute accuracy globally

scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: Reading-A exemplar. Credited by author's own qualitative standard (the formula reproduces observations). Non-discriminating: any geometry that preserves the observer–sun angular relationship reproduces elevation. The λ_g scale "reused elsewhere" is the dome's fitted constant, not an independent mechanical law.

globe.value: Superluminal phase velocity (~1.57c) in the Earth-ionosphere waveguide at ELF, verified by Tesla's 1899 Colorado Springs measurements and reproduced in modern Schumann-cavity theory.
globe.source: Tesla US Patent 787,412; Schumann 1952; modern ELF waveguide analyses (Bliokh 1980).

dome_published.value: 1.574c (derived from Tesla's 0.08484 s period divided by ECM disc circumference)
dome_published.quote: Tesla's 0.08484 s period is mapped to the ECM diameter to derive a 1.574c one-way longitudinal mode constant.

formula_check.value: 1.574c (v = (2 × 20,015 km disc radius × π) / 0.08484 s; numeric check passes)

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: Dome disc diameter 40,030 km equals globe circumference 40,075 km within 0.1% — the λ_g profile was set to reproduce WGS84 distances (see WIN-069). The 1.574c numerical agreement reproduces Tesla's measured value BY CONSTRUCTION because the disc circumference IS the globe circumference. Tesla's own patent diagram shows a spherical Earth; 1.574c is a waveguide effect requiring spherical geometry.

observation.value: ~1.57c superluminal phase velocity (Tesla 1899 Colorado Springs; Schumann cavity modern measurements)

scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: SESSION-CONTEXT / CLAUDE.md Kernel-of-Truth exemplar. Credited by own standard. Non-discriminating: the numerical agreement is by construction; Tesla's 1.57c is real waveguide physics requiring the very geometry the dome disputes.

globe.value: Sun appears LARGER at perihelion (~Jan 4), SMALLER at aphelion (~Jul 4). Angular diameter range: 31.46' to 32.53' (ratio ~1.034).
globe.source: Standard heliocentric orbital mechanics; Meeus; photometric observations.

dome_published.value: Model predicts seasonal direction OPPOSITE to observed (author flags as open-favors-globe)
dome_published.quote: The live site openly states that the observed seasonal direction currently favors the globe and remains unresolved in ECM's favor.

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: The dome's sun is at fixed altitude 5,733 km moving in a small circle; the disc-geometry prediction is a direct consequence of sun-altitude / observer-distance geometry and is genuinely dome-native.

observation.value: Sun larger at perihelion (confirmed; unambiguous across any standard photometric record)

scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: Discriminating and wrong — the rare row that is BOTH genuinely dome-native AND publicly counted against the model by the author himself. This is the scoreboard's positive proof that the author sometimes accepts adverse data, and it is the strongest single-row evidence that the dome's native discriminating predictions fail when they exist.

globe.value: Quiet-day null: no SG gravity anomaly contact-phase-locked beyond instrument systematics (~5 µGal); no B/g coupling at the claimed 1.677 nT/µGal ratio.
globe.source: IGETS quiet-day baselines; INTERMAGNET during eclipse conditions; Chapman-Sq nominal.

dome_published.value: BLANK — the author has stated a 3-tier protocol with "explicit success criteria and explicit falsification criteria" (claim_index.json) but HAS NOT published the specific Tier-3 numerical threshold or the operational κ-measurement protocol. NO SHA-256-anchored prediction document exists separately timestamped before Aug 12 2026 as of 2026-04-20.
dome_published.quote: Prospective August 12 2026 three-tier eclipse protocol with explicit success criteria and explicit falsification criteria.

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: Tier-3 depends on κ, a dome-native coupling constant. Forward-dome-native on paper — but the specific numerical threshold has not been published.
scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: PRE-REGISTRATION SURFACE. The scoreboard publishes this row with BLANK Tier-3 threshold cells before August 2026. If the cell is still blank on August 11 2026, the strict_prospective_confirmed claim was not operationalised in time — that is itself a finding. Cross-links to Timestamp Error tab: independent OTS-timestamping of the prediction document (not the observation) is the canary fix. Author has a VISIBLE place here to post the specific threshold + OTS-anchored prediction file.

globe.source: Geoscience Australia cadastral survey; OpenStreetMap distance audit.

dome_published.quote: V13 Finsler metric recovers 4,352 km Sydney-Perth surface distance from the two-zone λ_g profile.

formula_check.value: 4352 km (V13 Finsler λ_g fit reproduces Sydney-Perth distance by construction)

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: CANONICAL FIT ROW. Sydney-Perth observed distance was an INPUT to the λ_g parameter fit (see EXP-085, EXP-091, WIN-069 supporting data). The formula then "predicts" the input. Math-wrong CASE B. See also Part 8b.2 sinuosity analysis.
scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: Dome badge was demoted 2026-04-17 from CONFIRMED → STRUCTURAL SUPPORT (EXP-188). Scoreboard reflects current post-demotion dome framing. Provenance:fit is the load-bearing finding.

globe.value: Polaris altitude matches observer geographic latitude to arcminute accuracy at all northern latitudes; altitude → 0 at equator; Polaris invisible south of ~2°N.
globe.source: Standard spherical astronomy; IAU SOFA; any ephemeris.

dome_published.value: qualitative: "systematic excess altitude at high N" (WIN-065); falsifier was previously "Polaris visible south of equator"; post-window framing now demoted from CONFIRMED to SUPPORTIVE ONLY (EXP-188)
dome_published.quote: WIN-065 systematic excess. Pre-demotion kill-shot framing: "Polaris observable south of equator would falsify the model."

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: Provenance became unclear when the author demoted the kill-shot badge. Pre-demotion the claim was a discriminating falsifier; post-demotion the claim is "supportive only", which is a weaker, non-discriminating framing.

observation.value: Polaris invisible south of ~2°N (no observations from Southern Hemisphere observatories of Polaris above horizon); altitude at northern stations matches latitude to <1 arcmin.
observation.source: Global astronomical observatories; amateur astronomer network

scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: The kill-shot was rewritten mid-review. Post-demotion status makes the WIN-by-own-standard status ambiguous — hence "disputed". Dome author changed the rules; scoreboard records both pre- and post-window text in source field.

globe.value: Standard eclipse thermodynamics: energy-budget anomaly of order photospheric-insolation × umbral-area × totality-duration; no unexplained residual above Chapman-Sq ionospheric response.

dome_published.value: BLANK — no published numerical energy-budget threshold. Author cites "explicit success criteria and explicit falsification criteria" but the discriminating numerical threshold has not been publicly committed.
dome_published.quote: Pre-registered Tier-3 threshold referenced; specific number not in published materials as of 2026-04-20.

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: Duplicates pre-registration surface behaviour of pred-eclipse-tier3. Author has multiple overlapping eclipse-related claims that need row-level deconfliction.
scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: BLANK cell is itself a finding. Parallel to pred-eclipse-tier3; scoreboard row flags that the author has TWO eclipse kill-shot framings (Tier-3 magnetic/gravity vs energy-budget) and has not numerically published either. Pre-registration surface.

globe.value: Observed NMP drift 1990–2020 averaged ~55 km/yr; anisotropic ratio longitudinal:latitudinal ~1.4 under standard magnetic-field secular variation models (IGRF).
globe.source: IGRF-14; WMM 2020; NOAA NGDC NMP track.

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: Need to determine from V51.1 materials whether 2.26 is a forward prediction from dome parameters or a fit to observed drift data. Flagged for Phase 2 deep-dive.

observation.value: NMP longitudinal:latitudinal drift ratio computed from NOAA NGDC tracks 1990–2020
observation.source: NOAA NGDC NMP positions; IGRF-14 vs. observation residuals

scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: Provenance unclear blocks definitive scoring. Author should be queried whether 2.26 is forward or fit. Phase 2 deep work item.

globe.value: SAA is well-characterized by IGRF with multiple minima and secular drift; asymmetric decay of the African cell is predicted by the geomagnetic main-field model.

dome_published.value: qualitative: African cell decays "faster" (WIN-005); magnitude bound "< 21,795 nT" (WIN-035)
dome_published.quote: African SAA cell faster decay; SAA African < 21,795 nT.

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: WIN-035's 21,795 nT appears to be a threshold extracted from IGRF/SWARM data. Classification provisional — Phase 2 forensic.

observation.value: Compatible with < 21,795 nT threshold per IGRF-14 current-epoch maps

scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: Dome's SAA cluster (WIN-004, WIN-005, WIN-035, WIN-040, WIN-060, WIN-063) is large but every row is either qualitative or a threshold. No single SAA row gives a discriminating numerical dome prediction vs. IGRF.

globe.value: Observed JFK→LHR vs. LHR→JFK asymmetry is fully explained by northern-hemisphere polar/jet stream winds (average ~40-80 knots eastward aloft). Commercial ATC tracks reproduce the asymmetry quantitatively from NOAA GFS wind fields.
globe.source: NOAA GFS reanalysis; FAA ATC track archives; standard aviation meteorology.

dome_published.value: qualitative: "aetheric slipstream" generates JFK-LHR vs LHR-JFK timing asymmetry; no specific minutes/km dome prediction publicly archived
dome_published.quote: aetheric slipstream mechanism for JFK-LHR asymmetry

scoreboard_overrides.provenance_note: Qualitative dome-native mechanism; no quantitative commitment.

observation.value: Average JFK→LHR block time ≈ 6h45m; LHR→JFK ≈ 7h45m; asymmetry ~60 min; fully reproduced by GFS wind reanalysis with no aetheric residual.
observation.source: BTS aviation statistics; GFS wind reanalysis

scoreboard_overrides.row_notes: Asymmetry exists (observation confirms). But author's claim is qualitative, and globe aviation meteorology fully reproduces the timing residual. Non-discriminating. Win-by-own-standard disputed because "aetheric slipstream" has no numerical target to confirm against.

new_tab_html.insertion_point: top-level tab between Overview and Part 4 (Live Power Dashboard); homepage nav updated to direct first-time readers here before the narrative
new_tab_html.intro_html:
Scoreboard: Both-Model Predictions, Side by Side

We take the dome author at his word. He says each WIN is a confirmed prediction. We write the prediction and the observation side by side, using the author's own published values — never deriving them for him. Where the author has not published a specific prediction, the cell stays blank, because a prediction that was never made cannot have been confirmed.

How to read this table. Each row is one WIN, prediction, or kill-shot. Three numbers matter per row: the globe prediction (from a named authoritative reference), the dome prediction as the author published it, and the observation. From these we compute two verdicts: WIN-by-own-standard (did the author's published dome value match observation, by his definition of a WIN?) and Discriminating (did the dome's published value differ from the globe's prediction?).

Guardrail (load-bearing). Where the dome author has not published a numerical prediction, the cell is blank. We do not compute dome predictions using standard physics and score them against the author — that would be us constructing his predictions using the very framework his model disputes. The blank speaks for itself: by the author's definition of a WIN (a confirmed prediction), a missing prediction is not confirmable. We invite the author to populate dome predictions for any blank row; we will update the Scoreboard and date-stamp the additions.

Provenance annotations. Each filled dome cell carries a provenance badge: forward_dome_native (dome mechanics alone produced the number), forward_with_globe_inputs (standard-physics inputs with a dome-specific transform on top — legitimate if the transform is non-trivial, globe-polluted if it isn't), fit (the observation was an input to the parameter fit, not a prediction), or unclear (cannot tell from published materials). Provenance informs the reader; it does not alter the score. A row can be WIN-by-own-standard and provenance=fit simultaneously, and the reader can draw their own conclusions.

Headline at Phase 1 publication (computed from data at build time). Of {{TOTAL_WINS_PLUS_PREDS_PLUS_KILLSHOTS}} tracked rows, the dome author has published a specific numerical prediction for {{SCOREBOARD_FILLED}}. Of those {{SCOREBOARD_FILLED}}, {{SCOREBOARD_WIN_BY_OWN_STANDARD_YES}} match observation by the author's own standard. Of those, {{SCOREBOARD_DISCRIMINATING_YES}} are distinct from the globe's prediction. The remaining {{SCOREBOARD_BLANK}} rows have no published dome prediction and cannot be WINs under the author's own definition.

Related. Full per-row kill-shot narrative continues in Part 5 (Kill-Shot Analysis). Live Power Dashboard remains in Part 4. For the canary/OTS-timestamping methodological gap, see the Timestamp Error tab. Registry framing context (API-vs-visible-layer) is in §4.2 Registry Framing Audit (EXP-183).

{{SCOREBOARD_TABLE}}

Table generated from wins.json, predictions.json, and killshots.json at build time. Phase 1 publishes 15 rows; Phase 2 extends to the full row set as sourcing completes. See monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-190.json for the foundational draft spec.

table_generator_contract.generator_location: build-scripts/generate-html.js new helper generateScoreboardTable(wins, predictions, killshots)
table_generator_contract.sort: row_id ascending by default; sortable columns on the front-end are out of scope for Phase 1
table_generator_contract.blank_rendering: — with tooltip "no published dome prediction for this row"

formula_scripts_skeleton.README_content:
# Scoreboard Formula Implementations

Reviewable JavaScript implementations of V51.1 formulas that produce dome predictions.
Each script corresponds to a single scoreboard row's `dome_prediction_formula_check_value`.

## Conventions

- ONE formula per file.
- Each file cites the V51.1 section/equation it implements in its header.
- Each file exports a single pure function.
- Each file has a sibling `.test.js` with unit tests.
- Each file declares `inputs_are_dome_native: boolean` — TRUE iff the formula consumes only dome-parameter inputs (disc radius, λ_g, firmament height), FALSE if it consumes globe-native inputs (observed angles, measured distances, standard indices).
- Curmudgeon reviews every new script adversarially before the corresponding row is published.

## Current scripts

- `v13-finsler-sydney-perth.js` — V13 Finsler metric for Australia scaffold corridor (demonstrates provenance=fit).
- `theta-east-longitude.js` — θ = −lonE polar-azimuthal identity (demonstrates near-identity transform detection).

## Rounding tolerance

Each row's `rounding_tolerance_applied` field (e.g., `"1%"`, `"±10 km"`) is the pass/fail
threshold for `formula_reproduces`. Default is 1% relative OR stated absolute — whichever is
tighter.

scripts.name: v13-finsler-sydney-perth.js
scripts.summary: V13 Finsler λ_g profile implementation for Australia scaffold; demonstrates provenance=fit on Sydney-Perth.

scripts.name: v13-finsler-sydney-perth.test.js
theta-east-longitude.js
scripts.summary: θ = −lonE polar-azimuthal identity; demonstrates near-identity transform detection (globe-pollution flag).

scripts.name: theta-east-longitude.test.js

test_harness_integration.description: The main test.js should glob build-scripts/scoreboard-formulas/*.test.js and require each.
test_harness_integration.suggested_patch: Add to test.js `runFormulaTests()` helper: `const fs=require('fs');const path=require('path');const dir=path.join(__dirname,'build-scripts','scoreboard-formulas');if(fs.existsSync(dir)){fs.readdirSync(dir).filter(f=>f.endsWith('.test.js')).forEach(f=>require(path.join(dir,f)))}`

integration_points.part5_kill_shots: Each Part 5 kill-shot narrative section adds a "Scoreboard row" link in its summary; each scoreboard kill-shot row cross-links back to its Part 5 narrative. Scoreboard becomes the authoritative per-row score; Part 5 remains the narrative. No content in Part 5 is removed.
integration_points.part4_live_power_dashboard: Scoreboard is a sibling layer; Part 4 continues. Homepage nav updated to route first-time readers to Scoreboard before Part 4.
integration_points.timestamp_error_tab: pred-eclipse-tier3 scoreboard row links to Timestamp Error tab (which documents the OTS wrong-side error and the canary). Cross-ref from scoreboard row_notes explains that a properly OTS-timestamped Tier-3 prediction document would fill the currently-blank dome cell before August 12 2026 and would fix both the pre-registration gap and the canary.
integration_points.exp_183_author_fields: Scoreboard's per-row display can surface author_claim_class (from EXP-183) next to the score cells as a context annotation. The two schemas are parallel-but-distinct: author_* describes how the author frames the claim; scoreboard describes how the claim scores against observation. Keep files separate; do not merge.
integration_points.timestamp_error_canary_preservation: The scoreboard DOES NOT fix the canary. The canary requires the author to publish a separate, independently OTS-timestamped prediction document before the data arrives. The scoreboard's blank cells are a pre-registration surface — an invitation — but filling them with a number on our scoreboard is not a substitute for the author's own timestamped prediction artefact.

anticipated_objections.objection: (1) You're reducing cosmology to a scoreboard; theories are evaluated on explanatory unity, parsimony, and theoretical virtues — not just row-by-row matches.
anticipated_objections.response: The author framed the 69 WINs as 69 confirmed predictions. The scoreboard evaluates the object the author put on the table. Theory-level evaluation is a separate conversation; the scoreboard does not preclude it, it just audits the claimed-predictions layer honestly. Part 4 (Live Power Dashboard) and the broader narrative still address explanatory unity.

anticipated_objections.objection: (2) You demand numerical dome values for every claim, but models include qualitative predictions.
anticipated_objections.response: The schema includes a `dome_prediction_as_published_is_qualitative` marker. Where the author has made a qualitative prediction with no numerical target, the cell renders as "qualitative: <quote>" rather than blank. Scoring collapses to "phenomenon observed? yes/no" + "globe also predicted the phenomenon? yes/no." The author does not get to retrofit qualitative status onto claims he previously numericalized elsewhere; if any dome publication has a number, that number is the prediction.

anticipated_objections.objection: (3) Your globe numbers are wrong, therefore the whole scoreboard is impeached.
anticipated_objections.response: Globe values are sourced from named authoritative references (IGRF-14, Chapman 1931 + Hargreaves 1979, IAU SOFA, standard geodesy). Disputes are welcomed and handled per-row: `row_notes` records "author disputes our IGRF-14 lookup; under-review". The scoreboard survives individual-row disputes because each row is independently sourced. No single wrong globe number impeaches the table.

anticipated_objections.objection: (4) You're forcing me into a binary I didn't commit to — not every claim is win-or-lose.
anticipated_objections.response: The scoreboard has TWO outcome columns (win_by_own_standard, discriminating) with FIVE possible values each (yes/no/blank/pending/disputed). `row_notes` carries narrative. We do not collapse to a binary; we expose the structure. A row can be WIN-by-own-standard=yes AND discriminating=no AND provenance=fit — and the reader sees all three at once.

anticipated_objections.objection: (5) You didn't fill every dome cell either — your scoreboard is as incomplete as mine.
anticipated_objections.response: We explicitly do NOT fill cells the author did not publish. That is a design invariant, stated publicly in the intro paragraph. The blanks are the author's to fill; we will update and date-stamp any additions. Our scoreboard is complete at the task of "score every published dome prediction against observation"; the blanks are the author's absences, not ours.

anticipated_objections.objection: (6) You're using standard physics to derive dome predictions and then comparing them to observations; the comparison is rigged.
anticipated_objections.response: Rules 1-2 and Guardrails G1-G5 explicitly prohibit this. Dome values we derive from globe physics never enter the score cells. They appear only in `dome_prediction_formula_check_value`, clearly labelled, with an `inputs_are_dome_native` flag that trips when the formula is globe-polluted. The intro paragraph states this invariant verbatim. If any reviewer can point to a row where a derived value entered a score cell, that row fails curmudgeon review before publication.

anticipated_objections.objection: (7, curmudgeon-side) The review is 90% blank cells; this looks like we're just listing the unconfirmed rows to embarrass the author.
anticipated_objections.response: The blanks ARE a finding. Each represents a WIN-claim without a published prediction. We do not embellish or moralize in the row itself; the blank and its footnote are the entire argument. The scoreboard's honest report of a filled-vs-blank ratio is accurate, not polemical. The INVITATION ("we will update and date-stamp any additions") is genuine — if the author fills cells, the ratio changes.

anticipated_objections.objection: (8, extra) The dome author will say "win_by_own_standard=yes but provenance=fit is a contradiction — you can't credit and discredit the same row."
anticipated_objections.response: It is not a contradiction; it is the structure of the claim. Credit is for the author's criterion ("the published value matches observation"). Provenance is a methodological annotation ("the value was an input to the fit, not a prediction"). Both are true simultaneously; the reader interprets. This is more honest than collapsing to a single verdict that hides one of the facts.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author's April 2026 rigor push is real work: methodology.json formalises a 5-class claim taxonomy; claim_index.json classifies nine claims with provenance-like labels (including retrospective_structural, prospective_confirmed, open_favors_globe); honest_caveats admit ionospheric Sq competition on WIN-068 and independent-reproduction dependence on WIN-058-kappa. The author is moving toward the kind of operational clarity this scoreboard formalises. We are not inventing a discipline he is refusing; we are completing one he has begun.
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_: The scoreboard risks being read as "only the rows we chose to score" — Phase 2 publication with transparent row-selection criteria mitigates this.
A wrong globe number in even one cell impeaches the whole scoreboard rhetorically. Mitigation: every globe value carries a named authoritative source; disputes are per-row and visible.

A wrong formula-check implementation publishes a false methodological flag. Mitigation: all formula scripts require curmudgeon pre-review and unit tests; Phase 1 limits formula-checks to simple cases.

The "forward_with_globe_inputs" provenance label is load-bearing AND subjective — whether a transform is "near-identity" requires judgement. Mitigation: per-row explanation in provenance_note; curmudgeon adversarially challenges each claim.
Phase 2 cadence risk: rushing Phase 2 at the cost of correctness impeaches Phase 1 retroactively. Mitigation: scope Phase 2 as 4-6 week effort; rate-limit per-row work to analyst throughput.
out_of_scope_for_phase_1: CSS/design treatment for the Scoreboard tab beyond a functional table.
Interactive filters/sort on the table (static HTML is sufficient for Phase 1).
Machine-readable exports (JSON/CSV) of the scoreboard for third-party audit.
Historical version control of per-row edits (git log suffices for Phase 1).
Integration with the dome's own API (if they publish updates to claim_index.json, we update our scoreboard). Future work.
canary_status: The scoreboard is DESIGNED TO BE canary-transparent. pred-eclipse-tier3 row carries the blank Tier-3-threshold cell as a visible pre-registration surface. If the author responds by publishing the threshold + an independently OTS-timestamped prediction document, the canary fix is visible on this scoreboard. If the author responds by filling our scoreboard cell but NOT publishing a separate OTS-timestamped document, the canary gap remains and the Timestamp Error tab still stands. The scoreboard does not replace the canary; it exposes it.

exp_files: EXP-183 (author_* wins.json schema extension — parallel but distinct)

newsworthy: Dome review adopts author's own definition of a WIN — for 60 of his 69 WINs the prediction cell is blank, because he never published one.
newsworthiness_caveat: DO NOT publish this headline until after integration. Scoreboard Phase 1 is DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW.
tldr_evidence: We take the author at his word: a WIN is a confirmed prediction. Of 15 Phase-1 rows scored side-by-side (globe vs dome-as-published vs observation), [dynamic count] filled-cells match observation by author's standard, [dynamic count] are discriminating vs globe, and [dynamic count] have no published dome number at all — the author's own materials leave those cells blank.
tldr_verdict: The scoreboard is not "our methodology imposed on his work" — the rules come from his own definition of a WIN. Where cells are blank, the absence is his. Where cells are filled and provenance is `fit`, the "prediction" is the input relabelled. Phase 1 demonstrates the pattern on 15 rows; Phase 2 extends it.
holes_addressed: Review architecture previously had no single row-level surface where "globe prediction / dome prediction-as-published / observation" coexisted per claim.
Timestamp Error tab argues about methodology (OTS wrong-side) but did not have a concrete per-row consequence surface; pred-eclipse-tier3 scoreboard row is now that surface.
EXP-183 extended wins.json with author_claim_class but did not score claims against observation; scoreboard is the parallel layer that does.
Kill-Shot tab was demoted by author (EXP-188) to "strongest-evidence arguments"; scoreboard recentres on per-row scores, decoupling kill-shot rhetorical weight from row-level accountability.
new_evidence_added: claim_index.json / methodology.json / ai_manifest.json verbatim references per row.
Explicit fit-row identification (Sydney-Perth, Australia scaffold) with EXP-085/091 archival cross-link.
Globe-pollution G2 guardrail encoded in formula scripts (dome_native flag + isNearIdentityTransform helper in θ=−lonE implementation).
Pre-registration surface for pred-eclipse-tier3 (Aug 12 2026 Tier-3 threshold).

completion_signal.reason_not_consumed: HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-PIPELINE-ROUTING-001 explicitly directs: "Do NOT delete or mark-consumed HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001 in the analyst notes file. The note stays pending until integration completes." This EXP is a DRAFT; the HNOTE remains pending for the duration of the human-group review cycle.
completion_signal.note_annotation_added: Added draft_produced_by_analyst_at=ISO-8601, draft_exp_id=EXP-190 fields to HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001 in monitor/analyst/human-notes.json, preserving status=pending.
completion_signal.decider_next_action: Route EXP-190 to curmudgeon priority-queue with REQUIRE_ADVERSARIAL_REVIEW per HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-PIPELINE-ROUTING-001 Step 2. Create tracking issue. Log change file. DO NOT integrate.

notes_for_human_review_group: Populated row content is best-effort analyst sourcing within one run. Values marked BLANK are not "we didn't have time to look" — they reflect analyst judgement that the author has not published a specific numerical dome prediction for that row, even after checking wins.html, model.html, claim_index.json, methodology.json, killshot.html, coordinates.html, predictions.html. Phase 2 will re-check each blank with deeper V51.1 PDF / audit.html / review-response.html / monitor.py source review before the row is promoted to filled.

The "dispute" verdict is used liberally in Phase 1 for rows where the author's framing changed mid-review (Sydney-Perth demoted; Polaris demoted; Mohe "confirmed_mechanism_contested"). Human group should decide whether "dispute" should be renamed "author-framing-unstable" or similar — the semantic content is that the author himself has moved the goalposts, not that we are hedging.

The ratio of filled-to-blank cells in Phase 1 is 9/15 (60%), which is HIGHER than the expected full-universe ratio; we selected the 9 claim_index-classified rows precisely because the author gave them explicit attention. Phase 2 ratio will be much lower. Human group: consider whether Phase 1 should intentionally include 2-3 randomly-selected unclassified WINs to avoid sampling bias.

The formula-scripts directory is proposed in-tree (build-scripts/scoreboard-formulas/). Consider whether it should instead live in a dedicated repo for easier independent audit — aviation-style "open code and open data" is the standard the scoreboard invites.
Eclipse row duplication: pred-eclipse-tier3 and killshot-eclipse-ebr overlap. Human group should decide whether to merge them or keep them as distinct rows with a cross-ref.
NMP and SAA rows are provenance-unclear in Phase 1. These are candidates for promoting or demoting to Phase 2 only — they are not ready to publish.

WIN-068 is a qualitative WIN but genuinely supportive (ensemble signal exists). Non-discriminating does not mean wrong — it means the dome claim is consistent with globe physics. The scoreboard should make this clear so non-specialist readers don't conflate "non-discriminating" with "refuted".
Timestamp Error tab cross-ref is load-bearing: without it, readers may misread the scoreboard as replacing the canary (it does not).
